name: gofer
displayName: Gofer for EnterpriseAI Vertical App Delivery [PREVIEW]
description: Guide repository work from business scenario to validated delivery inside VS Code, with EnterpriseAI-first workflow support when you want it.

commands.title: Gofer: Migrate Memories to Layered Format
Gofer: Execute All Pending Specs (Dependency Order)
Gofer: Pause Claude Code Terminal (Send ESC)
Gofer: Resume Claude Code Autonomous Monitoring
Gofer: Check for Slop (AI Code Quality)

viewsWelcome.contents:
Gofer helps turn a business scenario into a clear delivery path inside the repository: research, specification, planning, tasks, implementation, and validation.

No `.specify` workspace was found yet.

[Initialize Gofer](command:gofer.initialize)

A simple way to start:
1. Initialize the workspace
2. Open your AI chat/CLI and run `/0_business_scenario` (or `#0_business_scenario` in Copilot)
3. Review `proposal-review.md`, then continue through spec → plan → tasks → implement → validate

EnterpriseAI-first guidance is the default when you want that workflow. Set `gofer.workflowProfile=standard` only when you explicitly want the baseline Gofer workflow.

Use **Gofer: Install Optional Developer Tools** if your preferred CLI is not installed locally.

No AI usage data yet.

Usage will appear when AI API calls are logged.

view/item/context.when: viewItem == spec || viewItem == constitution-article || viewItem == constitution-section || viewItem == memory-document || viewItem == memory-section
viewItem == spec || viewItem == constitution-article || viewItem == constitution-section || viewItem == memory-document || viewItem == memory-section
viewItem == spec || viewItem == constitution-article || viewItem == constitution-section || viewItem == memory-document || viewItem == memory-section
viewItem == spec || viewItem == constitution-article || viewItem == constitution-section || viewItem == memory-document || viewItem == memory-section

gofer.anthropicApiKey.description: Anthropic API Key for Claude (get yours at https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys)
gofer.anthropicApiKey.markdownDescription: Your Anthropic API key for Claude. [Get one here](https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys). This is stored in your VS Code settings profile unless you choose another mechanism such as environment variables, and Gofer does not commit it to git.

gofer.googleApiKey.description: Google AI API Key for Gemini (get yours at https://aistudio.google.com/apikey)
gofer.googleApiKey.markdownDescription: Your Google AI API key for Gemini models. [Get one here](https://aistudio.google.com/apikey). Used by LLM Council feature for multi-provider execution.

gofer.openaiApiKey.description: OpenAI API Key for GPT models (get yours at https://platform.openai.com/api-keys)
gofer.openaiApiKey.markdownDescription: Your OpenAI API key for GPT models. [Get one here](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys). Used by LLM Council feature for multi-provider execution.

gofer.anthropicAdminApiKey.description: Anthropic Admin API Key for billing data access (starts with sk-ant-admin...)
gofer.anthropicAdminApiKey.markdownDescription: Your Anthropic **Admin** API key for billing/usage data. [Get one here](https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys). Required for the AI Token Usage panel to show real Anthropic spending. Admin keys start with `sk-ant-admin...`.

gofer.openaiAdminApiKey.description: OpenAI Admin API Key for billing data access
gofer.openaiAdminApiKey.markdownDescription: Your OpenAI **Admin** API key for billing/usage data. Required for the AI Token Usage panel to show real OpenAI spending. Needs the `api.usage.read` scope.

gofer.aiUsage.api.pollingInterval.description: How often to poll provider billing APIs for usage data (in milliseconds)
gofer.aiUsage.api.pollingInterval.markdownDescription: Polling interval for provider billing APIs in milliseconds. Default: 60000 (1 minute). Minimum: 15000 (15 seconds). Maximum: 300000 (5 minutes). Anthropic recommends polling no more than once per minute.

gofer.aiUsage.useApiClient.description: Use billing API client for usage data (set to false to revert to local JSONL)
gofer.aiUsage.useApiClient.markdownDescription: When `true`, fetches real billing data from Anthropic/OpenAI APIs. Set to `false` to revert to reading local `.specify/logs/council-usage.jsonl`.

gofer.autonomousMode.description: Enable autonomous monitoring for Claude Code terminal sessions
gofer.autonomousMode.markdownDescription: When enabled, Gofer monitors Claude Code terminal sessions and prepares context-aware responses when questions are detected. Requires `gofer.anthropicApiKey` to be set.

gofer.autoInitialize.description: Automatically offer to initialize Gofer when opening a repository without it

gofer.preferredAI.description: Preferred AI tool when sending tasks (ask, claude, copilot)

enumDescriptions: Standard mode — Claude Code asks for permission before executing tools
Yolo mode — Skip all permission prompts (--dangerously-skip-permissions)
Custom — Use the command specified in gofer.claudeCodeCommand
gofer.claudeCodeMode.description: How to launch Claude Code from the status bar
gofer.claudeCodeMode.markdownDescription:
Controls how Claude Code is launched from the status bar.

- **Standard**: `claude` — asks for permission before tools
- **Yolo**: `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` — skips all prompts
- **Custom**: Uses the command from `gofer.claudeCodeCommand`

gofer.claudeCodeCommand.description: Custom command to launch Claude Code (only used when mode is 'custom')
gofer.claudeCodeCommand.markdownDescription:
Custom command used when `gofer.claudeCodeMode` is set to `custom`.

Examples:
- `claude-skip` (shell alias)
- `/usr/local/bin/claude --flag` (full path with flags)

enumDescriptions: Use Claude Code CLI
Use Codex CLI
Use GitHub Copilot CLI (autonomous tasks use Claude/Codex fallback)
Use Gemini CLI (autonomous tasks use Claude/Codex fallback)
Auto-detect installed CLI
gofer.cliProvider.description: AI CLI provider for autonomous mode

enumDescriptions: Use Claude Code CLI for all Gofer commands
Use GitHub Copilot Chat for all Gofer commands
Use OpenAI Codex CLI for all Gofer commands
Use Gemini CLI command files for all Gofer commands
Auto-detect which CLI is installed and active
gofer.defaultCLI.description: Default AI CLI for Gofer command routing
gofer.defaultCLI.markdownDescription:
Choose which AI platform to use for Gofer slash commands (research, specify, plan, tasks, implement, validate).

- **Claude**: Use `.claude/commands/*.md` files
- **Copilot**: Use `.github/prompts/*.prompt.md` files
- **Codex**: Use `.system/skills/*/SKILL.md` files
- **Gemini**: Use `.gemini/commands/gofer/*.toml` files
- **Auto**: Automatically detect which CLI is active

This enables complete feature parity across Claude, Copilot, Codex, and Gemini.

enumDescriptions: Standard Gofer behavior when explicitly requested
EnterpriseAI-focused workflow guidance and artifact defaults
gofer.workflowProfile.description: Workflow profile used for Gofer pipeline guidance
gofer.workflowProfile.markdownDescription:
Select the workflow profile Gofer uses during pipeline execution.

- **enterpriseai**: Default EnterpriseAI-first guidance, templates, and artifacts
- **standard**: Existing baseline behavior when explicitly requested

This setting is additive and does not remove existing Gofer functionality.

gofer.enterpriseAiUseExternalReferences.description: Prefer external EnterpriseAI reference URLs during launch preflight instead of local fallback files
gofer.enterpriseAiUseExternalReferences.markdownDescription:
When enabled, Gofer uses external EnterpriseAI reference URLs during launch preflight before falling back to `.specify/references/eai/`.

When disabled, Gofer uses local fallback references directly.

gofer.codexCommand.description: Custom path to Codex CLI executable

enumDescriptions: VSCode's built-in Markdown Preview (read-only)
Mark Sharp - Fast WYSIWYG editor with live preview (requires extension: JonathanYeung.mark-sharp)
Markdown Editor - Feature-rich WYSIWYG editor (requires extension: zaaack.markdown-editor)
Markdown WYSIWYG - Simple WYSIWYG editor (requires extension: adamerose.markdown-wysiwyg)
gofer.markdownViewer.description: Choose how to view markdown files when clicking tree view items
gofer.markdownViewer.markdownDescription:
Choose how to view markdown files when clicking tree view items.

**Recommended WYSIWYG Extensions:**
- [Mark Sharp](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=JonathanYeung.mark-sharp) - Fast & modern
- [Markdown Editor](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=zaaack.markdown-editor) - Feature-rich
- [Markdown WYSIWYG](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=adamerose.markdown-wysiwyg) - Simple

gofer.autonomous.showTerminals.description: Show Claude Code terminals during autonomous execution
gofer.autonomous.showTerminals.markdownDescription:
Whether to show Claude Code terminals when Gofer runs autonomous implementation.

- `true`: Terminals are visible (you can watch progress)
- `false`: Terminals are hidden (cleaner UI)

gofer.autonomous.maxRetries.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for errors (1-5)
gofer.autonomous.maxRetries.markdownDescription:
How many times Gofer should retry when encountering recoverable errors.

- **Default**: 3 retries with exponential backoff (10s, 30s, 60s)
- **Minimum**: 1 (fail fast)
- **Maximum**: 5 (very persistent)

gofer.autonomous.tokenWarningThreshold.description: Show warning when context reaches this many tokens
gofer.autonomous.tokenWarningThreshold.markdownDescription:
Token count threshold for context window warnings.

**Default**: 150,000 tokens (75% of Claude's 200K context)

Gofer will show a warning when approaching this limit.

gofer.autonomous.tokenActionThreshold.description: Spawn new terminal when context reaches this many tokens
gofer.autonomous.tokenActionThreshold.markdownDescription:
Token count threshold for spawning a new terminal with fresh context.

**Default**: 180,000 tokens (90% of Claude's 200K context)

Gofer will automatically create a new terminal with a context summary when this limit is reached.

gofer.autonomous.compactionThreshold.description: Trigger context compaction at this percentage (50-95%)
gofer.autonomous.compactionThreshold.markdownDescription:
Context window usage percentage that triggers automatic compaction.

**Default**: 80% (160,000 tokens of 200K context)
**Range**: 50-95%

When context usage reaches this threshold, Gofer will automatically:
1. Summarize completed tasks to free up space
2. Preserve the last 10 tasks in full detail
3. Reduce context by approximately 40-60%

**Lower values** = more frequent compaction, smaller context
**Higher values** = less frequent compaction, larger context

gofer.autonomous.questionTimeout.description: How long to wait for user response to questions (milliseconds)
gofer.autonomous.questionTimeout.markdownDescription:
How long Gofer should wait for your response when it asks a question.

**Default**: 300,000 ms (5 minutes)

After this timeout, execution will pause and you'll need to manually resume.

gofer.autonomous.runFinalValidation.description: Run constitution validation check after implementation completes
gofer.autonomous.runFinalValidation.markdownDescription:
Whether to run a final validation check against the project constitution after completing implementation.

- `true`: Run validation (recommended)
- `false`: Skip validation (faster, but may miss issues)

gofer.autonomous.validateConstitution.markdownDescription:
Whether to validate implementation against the project's constitutional principles.

- `true`: Ensure compliance with constitution (recommended)
- `false`: Skip constitution validation

gofer.autonomous.enableEngineeringReview.description: Enable proactive engineering reviews during implementation
gofer.autonomous.enableEngineeringReview.markdownDescription:
When enabled, Haiku will proactively request engineering reviews at strategic milestones (typically 40-80% completion).

- `true`: Enable strategic reviews (recommended)
- `false`: Disable reviews (faster, but may miss issues early)

gofer.autonomous.enablePerformanceReview.description: Enable proactive performance reviews during implementation
gofer.autonomous.enablePerformanceReview.markdownDescription:
When enabled, Haiku will proactively request performance and architecture reviews near completion (typically >70% completion).

- `true`: Enable performance reviews (recommended)
- `false`: Disable reviews (faster, but may miss optimizations)

gofer.autonomous.engineeringReviewMinCom.description: Minimum completion % to trigger engineering review
gofer.autonomous.engineeringReviewMinCom.markdownDescription:
Engineering reviews will only be triggered when completion is at least this percentage.

**Default**: 40% (early enough to catch issues)

**Range**: 0-100%

gofer.autonomous.engineeringReviewMaxCom.description: Maximum completion % to trigger engineering review
gofer.autonomous.engineeringReviewMaxCom.markdownDescription:
Engineering reviews will only be triggered when completion is below this percentage.

**Default**: 80% (before polish phase)

**Range**: 0-100%

gofer.autonomous.performanceReviewMinCom.description: Minimum completion % to trigger performance review
gofer.autonomous.performanceReviewMinCom.markdownDescription:
Performance reviews will only be triggered when completion is at least this percentage.

**Default**: 70% (when core features are done)

**Range**: 0-100%

gofer.autonomous.engineeringReviewPrompt.default:
Please perform an engineering review of the work completed so far:

1. Compare the implemented code against the original specification
2. Identify any gaps or deviations from the spec
3. Check if the implementation aligns with the constitution principles
4. Verify that completed tasks match their requirements in tasks.md

Provide a brief summary of findings and recommendations for next steps.
gofer.autonomous.engineeringReviewPrompt.markdownDescription:
The prompt sent to Claude Code when requesting an engineering review.

**Default**: Asks Claude to compare implementation vs spec, identify gaps, check constitution alignment, and verify task completion.

**Tip**: You can customize this to focus on specific aspects of your project (e.g., security, accessibility, API design).

gofer.autonomous.performanceReviewPrompt.default:
Please perform a performance and architecture analysis of the implementation:

1. Review the code against current best practices for each technology used
2. Identify potential performance bottlenecks or optimization opportunities
3. Check for architectural patterns that could be improved
4. Verify proper error handling and edge case coverage
5. Suggest any improvements for maintainability and scalability

Provide specific, actionable recommendations.
gofer.autonomous.performanceReviewPrompt.markdownDescription:
The prompt sent to Claude Code when requesting a performance review.

**Default**: Asks Claude to review best practices, identify bottlenecks, check architecture, verify error handling, and suggest improvements.

**Tip**: You can customize this to focus on specific aspects (e.g., database queries, API performance, memory usage).

gofer.autonomous.haikuSystemPrompt.default:
You are an autonomous development assistant managing Claude Code's feature implementation workflow.

You have access to:
- The project constitution (principles and standards)
- The feature specification
- The implementation plan
- The task list
- The recent terminal output from Claude Code
- Latest best practices (you should consider current industry standards)

Your job is to monitor Claude Code and decide whether to interrupt based on the situation.

{WORK_STATE}

RESPONSE TYPES:

{RESPONSE_TYPES}

DECISION PRINCIPLES:
- If there's a question, answer it first (takes priority)
- Otherwise, decide next action based on completion percentage
- Keep work moving forward efficiently
- Reviews are checkpoints, not blockers - use them strategically
- Always align with constitution, spec, and plan

The goal is to autonomously drive feature completion with quality checks at appropriate milestones.
gofer.autonomous.haikuSystemPrompt.markdownDescription:
The system prompt that instructs Haiku on how to make decisions.

**Variables**: `{WORK_STATE}` (working vs idle state), `{RESPONSE_TYPES}` (available actions)

**Tip**: You can customize this to change Haiku's decision-making behavior.

**Warning**: Incorrect prompts may cause Haiku to make poor decisions.

gofer.observationPreservePatterns.description: Additional regex patterns for observations that should never be masked
gofer.observationPreservePatterns.markdownDescription:
Additional regex patterns (case-insensitive) for observation content that should **never** be masked during context management.

Built-in patterns already preserve: `error`, `exception`, `failed`, `failure`, `critical`, `fatal`, `panic`, `unhandled`, `stack trace`.

**Example**: `["timeout", "ECONNREFUSED", "segfault"]`

gofer.useLayeredMemory.markdownDescription:
Enable **MemGPT-inspired three-layer memory** architecture:
- **Core**: Always-loaded high-priority memories
- **Recall**: Recent session context
- **Archival**: Long-term searchable storage

When enabled, `buildContext()` uses layered memory instead of flat memory loading.

gofer.stageDetectionStalenessMinutes.description: Minutes before stage detection data is considered stale
gofer.stageDetectionStalenessMinutes.markdownDescription: How many minutes before the cached stage detection data (from `current-stage.json`) is considered stale and falls back to heuristic detection.

gofer.autonomous.haikuUserPromptTemplate.description: User prompt template for Haiku with context
gofer.autonomous.haikuUserPromptTemplate.markdownDescription:
The user prompt template that provides context to Haiku.

**Available variables**: `{terminalOutput}`, `{constitution}`, `{spec}`, `{plan}`, `{tasks}`, `{workState}`

**Tip**: You can customize this to include different context or formatting.

**Warning**: Incorrect templates may cause Haiku to miss important context.

gofer.yoloSlopReduction.enabled.description: Enable automatic slop reduction on file save
gofer.yoloSlopReduction.enabled.markdownDescription:
When enabled, Gofer automatically fixes common code quality issues ('slop') when you save a file:

- **console.log** statements are removed
- **debugger** statements are removed
- **@ts-ignore** is upgraded to `@ts-expect-error`

All fixes are logged to `.specify/logs/slop-reduction.jsonl` for audit.

**Note**: Only affects `.ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx` files. Test files are excluded.

gofer.yoloSlopReduction.notifyEvery.description: Show notification every N slop fixes (default: 10)
gofer.yoloSlopReduction.notifyEvery.markdownDescription:
How often to show a notification about slop reductions.

**Default**: Every 10 fixes

The notification shows the cumulative count of fixes in this session.

gofer.contextWindow.autoExecuteSave.description: Automatically execute /7_gofer_save when context reaches auto-save threshold
gofer.contextWindow.autoExecuteSave.markdownDescription:
When enabled, Gofer will automatically execute `/7_gofer_save` when context utilization reaches the auto-save threshold.

**Default**: Enabled (automatic context management)

**When enabled**: Automatically sends `/7_gofer_save` to Claude Code terminal
**When disabled**: Shows notification instead, requiring manual action

**Safety**: Only executes when a real Claude Code session is detected, not during testing.

**Tip**: Keep enabled for seamless context continuity without manual intervention.

gofer.contextWindow.autoSaveThreshold.description: Context utilization threshold for automatic session save (0.0-1.0, default: 0.65 = 65%)
gofer.contextWindow.autoSaveThreshold.markdownDescription:
The context utilization ratio that triggers automatic session save.

**Default**: 0.65 (65%)

**Range**: 0.0 to 1.0

**How it works**:
- Gofer monitors context usage continuously
- When utilization crosses this threshold, the auto-save event is triggered
- If `autoExecuteSave` is enabled, `/7_gofer_save` executes automatically
- If `autoExecuteSave` is disabled, a notification is shown instead

**Research-based**: 65% gives a safety margin before accuracy degradation begins. Going above 70% significantly degrades LLM accuracy.

gofer.contextWindow.autoResumeAfterSave.markdownDescription:
When enabled, automatically sends `/8_gofer_resume` to Claude Code terminal immediately after `/7_gofer_save`.

**Default**: Enabled (automatic resume after save)

**How it works**:
1. Context reaches auto-save threshold (65%)
2. `/7_gofer_save` executes automatically (saves session state)
3. `/8_gofer_resume` executes immediately after (loads fresh context)
4. Claude Code continues with clean context window

**Benefits**:
- Zero-interruption context continuity
- Automatic context refresh without manual intervention
- Maintains full session state across save/resume cycle

**Note**: Only works when `autoExecuteSave` is also enabled. Both commands execute in the same Claude Code session sequentially.

gofer.contextWindow.continuousSlopReduct.description: Enable periodic background workspace-wide slop scans
gofer.contextWindow.continuousSlopReduct.markdownDescription:
When enabled, Gofer periodically runs `SlopReducer.reduceWorkspace()` across the entire workspace.

**Default**: Disabled

This is disabled by default because repeated whole-workspace scans across many open VS Code windows can add cumulative CPU and disk pressure. Manual and save-triggered slop reduction paths remain available.

gofer.contextWindow.continuousSlopReduct.description: Interval in milliseconds for background slop scans
gofer.contextWindow.continuousSlopReduct.markdownDescription:
How often Gofer runs the background workspace-wide slop scan when `continuousSlopReduction.enabled` is turned on.

**Default**: 120000 (2 minutes)
**Range**: 60000-1800000

enumDescriptions: Log scope violations to console only — no user-facing warnings
Show warnings when agents access protected files — recommended default
Block file access that violates protected boundaries — strictest mode
gofer.scopeGuard.mode.description: ScopeGuard enforcement mode for protected file boundaries
gofer.scopeGuard.mode.markdownDescription:
Controls how ScopeGuard enforces protected file boundaries defined in spec.md `## Protected Boundaries` sections.

- **advisory**: Violations logged to console only
- **warning**: Violations produce VSCode diagnostics (Information/Warning)
- **blocking**: Violations throw `ScopeViolationError` and prevent file access

All checks are logged to `.specify/logs/tool-audit.jsonl` for audit.

gofer.budgets.maxCostUsd.description: Maximum cost in USD per pipeline run before enforcement kicks in
gofer.budgets.maxCostUsd.markdownDescription:
Maximum cost in USD for a single pipeline run. When cumulative token cost exceeds this threshold, the configured enforcement mode activates.

Cost is estimated using per-provider token rates (Anthropic, Google, OpenAI).

gofer.budgets.maxTokensPerRun.description: Maximum total tokens (input + output) per pipeline run
gofer.budgets.maxTokensPerRun.markdownDescription: Maximum total tokens (input + output) allowed in a single pipeline run. Whichever limit (cost or tokens) is hit first triggers enforcement.

enumDescriptions: Log budget warnings but allow pipeline to continue
Reduce context aggressively when budget is exceeded
Stop pipeline execution when budget is exceeded
gofer.budgets.enforcementMode.description: How the cost budget is enforced when limits are exceeded
gofer.budgets.enforcementMode.markdownDescription:
Controls what happens when a pipeline run exceeds the configured budget.

- **advisory**: Warnings logged to console and status bar — pipeline continues
- **truncate**: Context is aggressively reduced to lower costs
- **blocking**: Pipeline execution halts until budget is reset

Budget events are logged to `.specify/logs/gofer-run-ledger.jsonl`.

gofer.memory.coverageThreshold.markdownDescription:
**Memory Coverage Threshold** for tiered context loading (Feature 029).

When memory coverage of task keywords meets or exceeds this percentage, research documents are skipped and memories are loaded exclusively, reducing context tokens by 30-60%.

- `30` (default): Skip research docs when 30%+ of task keywords are covered by existing memories
- Lower values: More aggressive memory-first loading
- Higher values: Research docs loaded more often

gofer.aiUsage.statusBar.enabled.description: Show AI usage cost in the status bar
gofer.aiUsage.statusBar.enabled.markdownDescription:
Show current session AI usage cost in the VSCode status bar.

- `true`: Status bar shows `$(dollar) AI: $X.XX` (default)
- `false`: Status bar item is hidden

Click the status bar item to see a detailed provider breakdown.

gofer.aiUsage.polling.interval.description: Polling interval in ms for AI usage updates (fallback when file watcher is inactive)
gofer.aiUsage.polling.interval.markdownDescription:
How often (in milliseconds) to poll for usage data updates.

This is used as a fallback when the file system watcher is not active.

**Default**: 3600000 (1 hour)
**Range**: 1000-7200000

gofer.diagnostics.resourceSnapshots.enab.description: Write lightweight Gofer resource snapshots to the output log
gofer.diagnostics.resourceSnapshots.enab.markdownDescription:
When enabled, Gofer periodically logs lightweight resource snapshots including RSS, heap usage, active handles, and uptime.

These snapshots help attribute future long-running VS Code slowdowns or crashes without adding significant overhead.

gofer.diagnostics.resourceSnapshots.inte.description: Interval in milliseconds for Gofer resource snapshots
gofer.diagnostics.resourceSnapshots.inte.markdownDescription:
How often Gofer logs a resource snapshot when diagnostics are enabled.

**Default**: 300000 (5 minutes)
**Range**: 60000-3600000

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run prepare-language-server && npm run rebuild-native && npm run package
scripts.prepare-language-server: cd ../language-server && npm run build && cd ../extension && rm -rf language-server && cp -r ../language-server language-server && rm -rf language-server/src
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
